headline: 34% target achieved; strong teacher engagement masks critical regional gaps and scale readiness concerns.
collection_status: 464 of 1,350 responses collected (34% completion). Lindi and Mara dominate with 167 combined responses (36% of total). Nine regions below 50 responses; Rukwa critically underrepresented at 17. Current pace insufficient to meet target without accelerated collection.
key_findings: Teacher cohort represents 79% of responses (451/464), heavily skewed toward lower primary (41% of sample). Leadership voices (heads, WEO, district officials) significantly underweighted—only 164 responses (35%).

Expansion priority dominates: 168 respondents (36%) prioritize expanding KiuFunza; 113 (24%) demand new programs. Only 73 (16%) focus on strengthening existing implementation—signals appetite exceeds capacity.

Scale readiness deeply misaligned with ambition: mean score 2.7/5 indicates stakeholders perceive moderate-to-low organizational readiness, yet 60% of priorities assume scaled delivery and policy integration.

Regional concentration risk: Lindi (19% of total) and top-4 regions account for 279/464 (60%). Shinyanga, Songwe, Singida, Rukwa collectively represent only 104 responses—insufficient for regional strategy validation.
flags: Scale readiness-ambition gap: Stakeholders want expansion (61% of priorities) but assess readiness at 2.7/5. Immediate diagnostic needed on capacity constraints before committing to scaling promises.

Leadership underrepresentation: Only 164 policy-facing respondents (WEO, DEO, REC, ARC, REO, district officials combined) vs. 451 teachers. District/regional decision-maker buy-in insufficient for policy integration priority to succeed.
Geographic validity threat: Five regions have <35 responses each. Data from Rukwa, Reo inputs (2), Arc (5) too thin for regional analysis; risks skewed recommendations favoring high-response zones.
recommendation: Redirect 40% of remaining collection effort to underrepresented regions (Rukwa, Singida, Songwe, Shinyanga, Mtwara) and deliberately oversample district/regional officials (target: 250+ by close). Simultaneously commission rapid capacity audit against top 3 expansion priorities to reconcile 2.7 readiness score with 61% expansion demand before finalizing scaling roadmap.
